parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write your answer in about 100 words.

tasks.lead: Read this email from your neighbour, Mrs Green, and the notes you have made.

stimulus.head: EMAIL
stimulus.body:
From: Mrs Green
Subject: A small favour

Hello,

I hope you are well. I'm going to visit my daughter next week, and I'll be away from Monday to Friday.

Would you be able to water my plants and feed my cat, Biscuit, while I'm away? Of course, I'll bring you back something nice!

Mrs Green

bullets: ask what Biscuit eats and how often
ask where she keeps the key
tasks.write: Write your email to Mrs Green.

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write your answer in about 100 words.

tasks.lead: You see this announcement in your school English-language magazine.

stimulus.title: SMALL THINGS THAT HELP OTHERS
stimulus.body: You don't have to be a superhero to help people! Write an article about the small ways young people can help their neighbours and community. The best articles will be published next month.

bullets: ways young people can help their neighbours
a time when you helped someone
how helping others makes you feel

tasks.lead: Your English teacher has asked you to write a story.
tasks.startSentence: When Petra heard a strange noise from the house next door, she decided to find out what it was.

stimulus.body:
Your story must begin with this sentence:

When Petra heard a strange noise from the house next door, she decided to find out what it was.
